title: Fantastic (for real) Company
pros: Excellent Work-Life Balance: Somewhat flexible remote and hybrid options that support real balance. Fantastic Benefits: Competitive health plans, generous PTO, wellness programs, and strong retirement offerings. Career Growth: Ongoing training, development programs, and opportunities for advancement. Supportive Leadership: Executive team that listens, communicates transparently, and genuinely cares. Modern, High-End Offices: Class A facilities that are comfortable, well-equipped, and professionally maintained. Strong Culture: Employee-focused, collaborative, and values-driven. Fast-Growing Company: Constant innovation and expansion mean new challenges and opportunities. And we are only just beginning...
cons: The base salary is in line with other firms, which may seem average to those just starting out. However, the bonus structure is robust and rewards performance, often resulting in higher total compensation. The fast-paced, high-growth environment may not be for everyone. It requires self-motivation and the ability to adapt quickly. Projects can be complex and require initiative and creativity. Support is there, but youâre expected to take ownership.

title: Excellent.

Incentive plans are extremely well contemplated, designed and implemented. The combination of elements above provides for a very stable and logical equation which is: hard work = rewarded success for the individual, the organization, and most importantly the clients we serve.
cons: Itâs a relatively large company. Itâs also a rapidly growing organization. As noted in the Pros section, there are a lot of systems, plans and teams in place. While they are well designed, it takes some time to read, learn and understand all that comprises the Ryan ecosystem.

title: Great Benefits, Leadership and Workload Issues Hold It Back
body: Great Benefits, Leadership and Workload Issues Hold It Back
pros: The company offers great benefits. The health, dental, and retirement packages are a strong selling point, and the vacation policy is fair. Company is great for new grads or those fresh in the industry.
cons: Lack of clear direction and leadership from unorganized management creates significant challenges. Processes and procedures often feel chaotic, which negatively impacts team workflows. The frequent acquisitions add to the instability. Advancement opportunities feel limited unless you're part of specific cliques within management. A work-life balance is consistently promoted, however, the workload handed to individuals and teams heavily contradict this.

title: One of the best companies to work for!
body: One of the best companies to work for!
pros: Not only is our CEO brilliant, but he is also caring and innovative. The people I work with are driven and motivated by helping our clients succeed. Ryan places a high emphasis on ethics and doing the right thing- in a non ironic sort of way. The company truly empowers its employees to make the right decisions and to feel safe when reporting issues. Not only this, but the whole company gets a week off during Summer if Ryan is named as one of the best places to work. Apparently, there hasn't been a summer in recent memory where that wasn't true.
cons: Somewhat arbitrary hierarchy of roles and titles. It probably made sense in the early days, but with new acquisitions, it could become a point of contention or team morale issue when legacy internal team members are lower ranked than incoming acquisition team members who may or may not have the same level of experience.

The benefits package offered by the company is solid. It has come to my aid numerous times, providing support and resources that have made a significant difference in my personal and professional life. Whether itâs health-related benefits, financial support, or work-life balance initiatives, the company has consistently shown that it values its employees. Lastly, the company provides ample opportunities for personal growth and development. I have been able to gain new skills, take on new challenges, and advance in my career. I truly feel that the company is committed to helping me grow and succeed.

title: Managers and Directors Only Care for Themselves Causing Negative Hostile Work Environment
body: Managers and Directors Only Care for Themselves Causing Negative Hostile Work Environment
pros: As a company, Ryan does offer some remote flexible work schedules depending on location. Good amount of PTO.
cons: Bad unscrupulous managers; negative work environment; hostile work environment; little training; overworked employees; takes 3-4 months to get approval to hire someone leaving departments heavily understaffed; salaried employees work 60+ hours a week; HR and executive staff say one thing and do another; HR/managers/directors/and principals protect favored employees from HR actions; good employees have been fired for speaking out against favored hostile employees; good employees have been driven out of departments and/or the company by hostile actions of management; little chance for advancement for unfavored employees; Ryan's vision is good but is not followed by nationwide teams.

title: The Land of Milk and Honey...
body: The Land of Milk and Honey...
pros: This is the company you want to work for, period. If you are a person who doesn't mind working, this is the place for you. They make sure you have EVERYTHING you need and some. Not only are they providing you with every opportunity and resource to excel at your job, but they are also incredibly involved in making sure you get trained and you feel like you apart of the Ryan family. The benefits are amazing, they are designed to truly care for you and your loved ones. Not to mention you get opportunities to volunteer and be apart of the community. There is nothing that this company doesn't offer, I truly believe that it is the land of milk and honey when it comes to employment.
cons: I have yet to find one. But I'll be happy to let you know if I do.

title: Great Place to Work
pros: Hybrid and flexible work hours, work life balance, incentives and health benefits are a few reasons why I've been working at Ryan for 20 years. Transparency by leader with monthly calls helps keep everyone informed on the spot. Leadership follows up on issues raised in these meetings. Great career opportunities to go as high up the ladder as you want.
cons: Working from home is not for everyone.

title: Great place to work!
pros: The people at Ryan are great -- very conscientious, helpful and fun to work with. There is great support for working remotely. And so many tools to help make work go smoothly.
cons: I've only been here a month or so, and haven't really seen any yet.

title: My 2nd Year at Ryan
pros: It's my 2nd year at Ryan and tenure did not affect my perception. I still think Ryan is a company with tremendous potential and rapid growth globally. Great opportunity for younger professionals starting a career, considering that as a company scaling up, you have plenty of growth opportunities. It's a company that values employee feedback and strives to keep up with the industry/market.
cons: As a company that is scaling up, sometimes you might feel overwhelmed with everything that is going on, however, this is something that can play to our advantage. The room for growth and learning is enabled by the dynamic environment that shapes this company.

title: Great Place to Work
pros: We have the BEST CEO. He is very ethical, business savy, fair, and values feed back from employees. In fact, he invites the entire organization on a call once a month and reads off every single question. The leadership team is invited on the call to comment on questions as well. We also have the BEST work/life balance in place that has been in place for about 15 years.
cons: After working at this organization for 15 years, I have no negative feedback. We are working hard at diversity and inclusion and have grown and evolved into a great workplace.

title: Great Place to Work!
pros: Great Benefits, MyRyan, Training, Opportunity!
cons: Hire more people so we can have a work load balance.

body: Remarkable place to work with strong belief in work/life success.
pros: There are strong women in leadership roles. The myRyan environment provides for a flexible work arrangement that allows me to volunteer, manage appointments, and family activities around my work day. There is a constant effort to improve processes and operations.
cons: Ryan can be an intense place--there are lots of overachievers and that can create mental pressure and anxiety about performance.

title: High Expectations and no work life balance
body: High Expectations and no work life balance
pros: This is a growing company, and the CEO is transparent and has monthly meetings to keep everyone informed. He even hosts Q&As at the end of the sessions. You have flexibility of working remote or going into the office when you choose to. It is also great that Ryan gives back to the community once a year, and team members get together for team building and at the same time giving back to their local community.
cons: As it says on Ryan's own website "Ryan employees are given the freedom to do their best work in the way they work best. With a clear understanding of expectations and accountabilities, employees have ownership of their time and flexibility to meet the demands of their professional and personal lives". However, this is not the case at all. Maintaining a healthy work-life balance is essential for personal well-being and job satisfaction. This company or at least the property tax department does not provide adequate support for work-life balance. Many employees feel burnout. This is a HIGH stress environment, and have caused many of the team members mental and physical health issues, and strained relationships with family and friends. You are provided with unlimited PTO but its likely you will never take PTO the only guarantied days you will take off are the 12 paid holidays, but even still, you will come back to work the next day and will be drowning with work.

title: The best company I've worked for until now
body: The best company I've worked for until now
pros: The work is exciting, the team is fantastic and overall, it's an extremely rewarding experience. I also like that I am treated like an adult and micromanaging is not encouraged. My peers are professionals that I can rely upon when reaching out for support, and I love helping out when someone needs any help. It's remote work, but done right. I don't feel disconnected or frustrated by the lack of interaction. I also appreciate the stability of the company, the true focus on employees and I love Brint, the founder of the company. His energy is contagious and inspiring. I am truly grateful for having the opportunity to add my contribution to Ryan.
cons: For my work, the reliance of Microsoft tools is a bit of a downer, since their tools are not the best for what I need, but this gets compensated by my department as I get access to all the tools I need in addition to Microsoft.

title: You won't find better benefits and people
body: You won't find better benefits and people
pros: Benefits, benefits, and benefits. This company is always reevaluating their benefits based on what the employees are wanting including salary comparisons for your area and your position. Sabbaticals, PTO, and insurance are also great things about this company. Even outside of benefits, the people that work here are great between co-workers, managers, and principles and directors. All of the higher ups take a very active roll in helping the many, many employees of the company. The flexibility the company provides allows for a true work life balance not just the promise of one but the company wants you to unwind and come back even stronger.
cons: There are always downsides to an organization; however, I have worked here for three years so far and plan to work many, many more. I would say the only thing I have noticed is that because people stay here so long and want to stay their is some favoritism as far as who gets what projects.

title: Definitely a Great Place to Work!
body: Definitely a Great Place to Work!
pros: There are numerous pros, but the two biggest are: 1) The myRyan program allows most roles the flexibility to work remote. 2) Management is very transparent - there are monthly team calls with the CEO who reviews financials as well as has an open Q&A. He is very honest and open when he answers the questions and if needed, he directs the appropriate leader to look into any question.
cons: I haven't seen any cons. As with every company there will be times when some employees are not happy. However, I have known many to leave seeking a better position, only to come back to work for Ryan.

title: Fantastic Experience - The Best!
pros: Too many to list them all but here are some top of mind: -Great culture -Huge Market and stability - recession resistance -Fantastic Talent -Pay and Benefits - best I've ever seen -Open and Honest Communication from the CEO down -Leadership Support is unmatched -Everyone pulls the same direction -best for client and firm - low politics -No layoffs - ever For People Leaders - -Supportive HR -Great talent identification - high potential talent pool of ICs -Not understaffed - Leadership support w/justification -Open door policy from the top down -Best in class onboarding and training for you and your team -Clear advancement path and resources for future leaders -Exec and Mentorship program -Meritocracy - Get what you put in - fair and transparent comp plan -Private company with major stability and heritage like a public firm
cons: Really not very many: -Growing pains that come with frequent M&A -Technology maturity not there yet - but we're getting there! -Like any large company - have to work hard to break down silos - right attitudes to work together but its an unavoidable challenge to align on ways of working

title: Outstanding Place to Work

This company truly cares about the health and happiness for each and every employee.
cons: Honestly, I can not think of any cons about this company. They are top notch

title: Amazing Leader!
pros: I've worked for Ryan under Brint's leadership for over 15+ years. He is an amazing leader and truly cares about his employees. Yes, you read that right "his" employees because you're not just a number. He takes great pride in ensuring the trajectory of the Firm always considers what is best for everyone not just his own interests.
cons: With a fast growing company, the growing pains are probably the biggest con. While it's to be expected, as an individual it can be a little stressful and taxing at times. I will say the benefits far out way the pains but I think feedback is important so I wanted to include at least one con otherwise it might seem too good to be true.

title: Overall a Good Start
pros: The overall compensation is lower base salary, but there is commission for completed files that's paid out monthly which is better than a yearly lump sum commission. The benefits are decent, and 3-weeks starting vacation is fairly on par with competition. Overall, as a global company they try to offer the same benefits across each region, and they're catching up in that regard. The presented culture is great and they offer really good benefits for personal/professional development reimbursement.
cons: The commission is meant to make up for lower base salary, but in the Consultant role you cannot control the files you're assigned, so while commission isn't 'capped' in theory, in practice you can't control what you work on (higher-value/hours worked files vs larger projects with less commission per hours worked) so you're likely capped in the $11-14k/year for commission. The Government Grants practice is about volume, meaning it can be stressful and this stress is pushed down from management to Consultants. The department is currently top-heavy which indicates slower growth potential as of writing.

title: Big fish in little pond
pros: Because the company is on the smaller side, you get to learn a variety of things. You aren't stuck doing 1 thing really well. More opportunities are available for learning new things. If you are willing to look outside your expertise there are other options available to you, should you wish it. It just requires communication on what you want and what you're looking for.
cons: Because of the smaller company there are only so many slots for advancement. So if its filled you will have to wait it out to progress within the same department depending on where you are.

pros: Great place to work if you want to work in a small team. You'll grow a lot in the transfer pricing industry and have an awesome work-balance. This is a niche industry so you'll get paid more than larger firms.
cons: The transfer pricing department is small so there aren't a whole lot of resources for professional development. Exit opportunities aren't great so if you're in transfer pricing, you'll stay in transfer pricing.

title: Donât Work Here If Youâre Black
body: Donât Work Here If Youâre Black
pros: The job experience was better when the old Superviser was in office. When she quit everything went downhill. Hybrid schedule - 1 week on/1 week off.
cons: Racism Discrimination Micro-aggressions Sexual Assault Bullying Micromanagement Ryan, formally known as Paradigm Tax Group is the worst company Iâve ever worked for. The executive board, management and even HR believe they are untouchable. They have bulldozed everyone in their path, regardless of the situation. Their main focus is protecting the company and they will cut you down to do so. I have been sexually assaulted and discriminated against while on the job. HR did nothing. If you are black, do not work for this company. Do not be fooled by the (3) black people in HR. They are for show. Aside from onboarding, you will be on your own as they are not allies. What I went through at Ryan, formally Paradigm Tax Group will haunt me for years to come. I am fighting back with a lawsuit so that justice will finally be served.

cons: Onboarding after old company was purchased was a pain. Working at home during the pandemic learning company policies was difficult. We were promised to have a Ryan buddy to help with transition; but did not happen. Only way to promote with my current experience is to get an accounting degree and CPA.

title: Only looking out for #1
pros: I had a fantastic team! We relied on one another and had each other's backs. I developed some great friendships that will last a lifetime.
cons: The leadership at Ryan is looking out for themselves and the trickle down effect is detrimental. I understand that tough decisions have to be made for the greater good of the company, but they make decisions with the outcome ensuring they stay in the limelight, regardless of who it pummels in the process. A company of their size sound not have top leaders in the weeds with approvals of everything (and I do mean everything). They have favorites and those folks are untouchable, no matter how many ethics complaints are made to HR about them. There are unethical practices in every department of the company with the mindset of "oh well, this is how we have always done it". I think everyone knows that people are afraid to give true feedback, so when it comes to the MANY survey's employees are asked to complete, it's best to just say everything is dandy and move on.

title: A company that puts its actions and money where its mouth is!
body: A company that puts its actions and money where its mouth is!
pros: Ryan markets its work environment during recruiting as an incredibly inclusive, positive, and flexible. All of these things are true. The flexibility of work location (WFH vs. In office vs. Hybrid) is great. In my specific area, I am allowed to WFH or in office. The technology sector of the company is exciting and growing quickly. Our software platforms are built in-house and offer exciting challenges in modernization and improvement.
cons: Probably a common issue elsewhere: politics/feelings occasionally getting in the way of software progress. In addition, getting approval for access to things such as certain databases is a lengthy process due to the sensitive nature of the data that we work with.

title: Good place to start career
pros: The company culture is great and you gain good experience. Its a great place to start your career. I was on a good team which very much helps. Company is recession/pandemic-proof and has never done any big layoffs.
cons: Just like most companies, they don't like to give significate raises which forces employees to look elsewhere or get pouched from competitors. Brint Ryan also is the final decision maker on EVERTYHING. While this might have worked when the company was smaller, with the current size and trying to become IPO ready, it will not work and really slows things down. Also, Brint does bribe his employees for good glassdoor reviews by giving everyone in the company a week off for "Summer Vacation".

title: Good Business, Bad for Employees
pros: The business model is actually spectacular for the partners as well as the clients. I felt that the customer delivery experience truly does exceed most others in the space and firm. The value billing vs. hourly billing proposition makes a lot of sense for them and allows the firm to focus on true issues for the clients rather than the attempt to just generate hourly fees.
cons: There is an above the law mentality within the firm. I don't have an issue when this translates to being a better advocate for clients (although watch out for opening a can of worms for audits). The same mentality translates to how they treat employees. I know of one past employee who they fired and denied unemployment for. He would have been able to fight for it, but they used scare tactics to prevent him from doing so. This mentality is used to prevent you from ever touching their clients. Non compete is general practice, and in general not enforceable. They use scare tactics and politics stating that all disputes must be resolved in Texas (they have huge amounts of political control) which isn't actually legal. It's just in the contracts to scare people. Unlike traditional accounting firms they aren't held to any real standards of ethics.

title: Overall great company!
pros: Corporate puts a lot of effort into employee satisfaction. They have enabled teams to work with more flexibility, rolled out new databases tools to help with efficient working, established a "summer break" week for all teams, and keeps constant corporate communication of what is going on in the company.
cons: There is a small window for raises and promotions. If that window is missed, regardless of validity of the merit, the person would have to wait an entire year to be recognized by title and/or compensation. Which leaves a lot of opportunity for good talent to walk out the door for a better offer from a recruiter. Although the company has some great policies for flexibility for work/life balance, much of it will depend on the team leader you are working with and the needs of the client. Keep in mind, this is a client service industry.

body: Every year is better than the last at Ryan.

I enjoy working with people who are at the top of their game. Leadership makes a point of ensuring employees are well versed in our mission and goals, which starts at the top. Our monthly Team Talk meetings are always informative along with a question and answer session at the end where employees share their thoughts and suggestions. CEO's motto is "Feedback is a Gift" and he actually models that behavior.
cons: I have no complaints. Any job has its cons if you look for them, but overall I enjoy my job at Ryan and feel appreciated.

title: Isolating work environment and low salary
body: Isolating work environment and low salary
pros: Flexible work environment and ability to work remotely. Benefits are ok. You have a good parental leave option if you have been at the job for at least a year. They contribute to your 401k and pay for a portion of your cell phone bill.
cons: For health insurance, you only have two options. You can either choose a HDHP or a PPO. The PPO is expensive and the out of pocket amount for one person is more than $2,500 a year which is a lot when the salary is already low. Depending on the team you are on, you may not have contact with anyone for weeks at a time besides submitting assignments. The work environment is busy and since you can work remotely and be busy all year, it can feel isolating. The starting salary even for individuals with graduate degrees are low. Depending on the team you are on, your bonus structure, that can be low as well. Also, their idea of help with student loan debt is telling you to refinance.

title: A Healthy Work Environment
pros: Ryan has great benefits and a strong focus on work/life balance. The managers I have encountered are respectful and encouraging.
cons: The pay is a little low compared to other consulting firms. They stress the value of their benefits make up the difference between the competing salaries, however at the end of the day the paycheck is still lower.

title: 9.5 out of 10
pros: I feel very blessed to work for this organization. I feel valued and respected, especially during a pandemic with countless unemployed individuals. Ryan continues to hire and has adapted quite well to working from home. I work with some amazing people and I consistently feel valued and respected. The company is well organized with clear plans for continued success and growth. I genuinely appreciate the CEO and Chairman taking time, monthly, to answer questions live from his employees.
cons: My complaints are few: 1. The 401k employer contribution is weak. 2. The main source for policies (RyanNet) has outdated policies and they are very poorly organized and difficult to find. 3. There have been times when Brint (CEO) makes an announcement or decision during his monthly talks that have changed or been revised after the call. I feel, sometimes, itâs best he answer with, âlet me look into that and Iâll make an informed decision soonâ instead of making unkept promises.

title: Hard to find a better place to work
body: Hard to find a better place to work
pros: Ryan has a great culture! The company is very people-focused, well blended with the needs of the business. Everyone is trusted to do the best job they can, and Ryan empowers everyone to contribute to achieving company and personal growth. The company proves that they care about their people with the many great benefits above and beyond medical and dental (which are great), like vision, wellness programs, counseling services, legal services, tuition reimbursement, and on and on. Mr. Ryan and the leadership team was well prepared to act quickly to the COVID-19 crisis, and was able to transition the firm, across the globe, to work remotely with very little hiccups and zero reduction in force; in fact, we continue to hire through it all. This is one of the best places I've worked in 30+ years, with some of the sharpest colleagues, and look forward to many more to come.
cons: I haven't personally experienced any in the several months I've been at Ryan.

title: Great Company
pros: I enjoy the autonomy that is offered at Ryan. Benefits are great. There is a lot of
cons: Certain local offices may have growing pains as turnover has been a problem.

title: Great Place to Work
pros: Transparency and timely communication by senior leadership. Management follows up on their words with effective actions; no fake or empty promises. Great work-life balance. I have worked at a few consulting firms where work-life balance is an idea or slogan pushed by HR, but leadership and managers don't follow through on it or it only applies to a select group of people. At Ryan, work-life is real and my family and I are so happy about it. Most of all, the people are great to work with. Ryan understands its people are its greatest resource and responsible for its success, and not just disposable bodies that are cut at the first sight of a missed quarter.
cons: Ryan is experiencing some growing pains, which can make certain processes frustrating. However, senior leadership is aware, has plans to improve them and is investing in the improvements.

The people who work here are nothing like anywhere else I have ever worked, which is a testament to strong leadership, starting with Brint Ryan. Beyond the people who work here, the purpose that the company aspires to goes beyond money/profit. There is a dedication to the primary purpose and an integrity to how we go about it. I am proud to work at Ryan and appreciate everything the company has done from me and my family.
cons: I don't have any complaints

title: Great place to work
pros: I have worked at Ryan for over 6 years and only wish I had started with Ryan earlier. Great flexibility, pay, and people. Upper management works hard to regularly communicate to the entire firm. A very positive culture and a firm that truly cares about its employees.
cons: None that I can think of.

title: Great Place to Work
pros: Employees are challenged to continually improve and to always provide excellent client service to each and every client. Has continued to keep everyone working during the pandemic and has continued to hire new staff.
cons: Sometimes the internal communication is too U.S. centric for an international company
